phase1.explanation:
Before writing Java code, you need three things:

1. JDK (Java Development Kit) — includes the compiler (javac) and runtime (JRE)
2. An IDE or text editor — IntelliJ, Eclipse, VS Code, or even Notepad!
3. Understanding the two-step process:
   • javac compiles .java files into .class bytecode
   • java runs the bytecode on the JVM

Think of it like cooking: javac is the prep work (chopping ingredients), java is the actual cooking (making the meal). The JVM is the stove that works with any kitchen!

Popular IDEs:
• IntelliJ IDEA — industry standard, very popular
• Eclipse — free, open-source, lots of plugins
• VS Code — lightweight, modern

Pro tip: Start with VS Code or IntelliJ Community Edition — they're free and great for beginners!
phase1.openingDialogue: Time to set up your workspace! Don't worry, I'll guide you through it step by step~ 🛠️

phase2.explanation: Conceptual lesson — no broken code yet! But a common mistake beginners make is downloading the wrong JDK version. Always check: 'java -version' and 'javac -version' in your terminal to verify both are installed correctly.

phase3.prompt:
What is the correct sequence to compile and run a Java program?

A) java → javac → run
B) compile → write code → run
C) javac → java
D) write code → javac → java
dialogueHints: What's the difference between javac and java?
phase3.openingDialogue: Let's check if you got the workflow right~ 🤔
